meanings.definition: the physical coming together of two or more things
examples: contact with the pier scraped paint from the hull

meanings.definition: the act of contacting one thing with another
examples: repeated hitting raised a large bruise
after three misses she finally got a hit

meanings.definition: deliver a sharp blow, as with the hand, fist, or weapon
examples: The teacher struck the child
the opponent refused to strike
The boxer struck the attacker dead

meanings.definition: have an emotional or cognitive impact upon
examples: This child impressed me as unusually mature
This behavior struck me as odd

meanings.definition: hit against; come into sudden contact with
examples: The car hit a tree
He struck the table with his elbow

meanings.definition: make a strategic, offensive, assault against an enemy, opponent, or a target
examples: The Germans struck Poland on Sept. 1, 1939
We must strike the enemy's oil fields
in the fifth inning, the Giants struck, sending three runners home to win the game 5 to 2

meanings.definition: indicate (a certain time) by striking
examples: The clock struck midnight
Just when I entered, the clock struck

meanings.definition: affect or afflict suddenly, usually adversely
examples: We were hit by really bad weather
He was stricken with cancer when he was still a teenager
The earthquake struck at midnight

meanings.definition: stop work in order to press demands
examples: The auto workers are striking for higher wages
The employees walked out when their demand for better benefits was not met

meanings.definition: touch or seem as if touching visually or audibly
examples: Light fell on her face
The sun shone on the fields
The light struck the golden necklace
A strange sound struck my ears

meanings.definition: attain
examples: The horse finally struck a pace

meanings.definition: produce by manipulating keys or strings of musical instruments, also metaphorically
examples: The pianist strikes a middle C
strike `z' on the keyboard
her comments struck a sour note

meanings.definition: cause to form (an electric arc) between electrodes of an arc lamp
examples: strike an arc

meanings.definition: find unexpectedly
examples: the archeologists chanced upon an old tomb
she struck a goldmine
The hikers finally struck the main path to the lake

meanings.definition: produce by ignition or a blow
examples: strike fire from the flintstone
strike a match

meanings.definition: remove by erasing or crossing out or as if by drawing a line
examples: Please strike this remark from the record
scratch that remark

meanings.definition: cause to experience suddenly
examples: Panic struck me
An interesting idea hit her
A thought came to me
The thought struck terror in our minds
They were struck with fear

meanings.definition: drive something violently into a location
examples: he hit his fist on the table
she struck her head on the low ceiling

meanings.definition: occupy or take on
examples: He assumes the lotus position
She took her seat on the stage
We took our seats in the orchestra
She took up her position behind the tree
strike a pose

meanings.definition: form by stamping, punching, or printing
examples: strike coins
strike a medal

meanings.definition: smooth with a strickle
examples: strickle the grain in the measure

meanings.definition: pierce with force
examples: The bullet struck her thigh
The icy wind struck through our coats

meanings.definition: arrive at after reckoning, deliberating, and weighing
examples: strike a balance
strike a bargain

meanings.definition: sensational in appearance or thrilling in effect
examples: a dramatic sunset
a dramatic pause
a spectacular display of northern lights
it was a spectacular play
his striking good looks always created a sensation

meanings.definition: having a quality that thrusts itself into attention
examples: an outstanding fact of our time is that nations poisoned by anti semitism proved less fortunate in regard to their own freedom
a new theory is the most prominent feature of the book
salient traits
a spectacular rise in prices
a striking thing about Picadilly Circus is the statue of Eros in the center
a striking resemblance between parent and child
